title: acanthospermum glabratum
intro: Acanthospermum glabratum, also known as cocklebur, is a type of flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and spiny fruit. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in reducing pain and inflammation. Acanthospermum glabratum has been reported to have various health benefits, including treating fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to make topical creams and ointments for skin irritations.
